Set user agent for GitHub API requirements\n            headers = {\n                'User-Agent': f'{REPO_OWNER}-{REPO_NAME}-release-fetcher',\n                'Accept': 'application/vnd.github.v3+json'\n            }\n\n            req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers)\n\n            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:\n                if response.status != 200:\n                    print(f\"Error: GitHub API returned status {response.status}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                    sys.exit(1)\n\n                data = json.loads(response.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n                if not data:\n                    break\n\n                releases.extend(data)\n\n                # Check if we've fetched all releases\n                if len(data) < per_page:\n                    break\n\n                page += 1\n                # Rate limit avoidance: small delay between pages\n                time.sleep(0.1)\n\n        except urllib.error.URLError as e:\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            print(f\"Error decoding JSON response: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Unexpected error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n\n    return releases\n\n\ndef is_correctness_release(release: dict) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Determine if a release is correctness-labeled based on tag name and release notes.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        True if release appears to be correctness/security-related\n    \"\"\"\n    tag_name = release.get('tag_name', '').lower()\n    name = release.get('name', '').lower()\n    body = release.get('body', '').lower()\n\n    # Check both tag name, release name, and body for correctness keywords\n    combined_text = f\"{tag_name} {name} {body}\"\n\n    for keyword in CORRECTNESS_KEYWORDS:\n        if keyword in combined_text:\n            return True\n\n    return False\n\n\ndef parse_release(release: dict) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a GitHub release into structured output.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary with {tag, published_at, is_correctness, url}\n    \"\"\"\n    return {\n        'tag': release.get('tag_name', ''),\n        'published_at': release.get('published_at', ''),\n        'is_correctness': is_correctness_release(release),\n        'url': release.get('html_url', ''),\n    }\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Main entry point.\"\"\"\n    # Fetch all releases\n    raw_releases = fetch_releases()\n\n    if not raw_releases:\n        print(\"No releases found\", file=sys.stderr)\n        print(json.dumps([], indent=2))\n        return 0\n\n    # Parse releases\n    parsed_releases = [parse_release(r) for r in raw_releases]\n\n    # Output as JSON (already sorted by GitHub API - newest first)\n    print(json.dumps(parsed_releases, indent=2))\n\n    # Print summary to stderr\n    correctness_count = sum(1 for r in parsed_releases if r['is_correctness'])\n    print(f\"Fetched {len(parsed_releases)} release(s)\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"Correctness-labeled: {correctness_count}\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"Routine version bumps: {len(parsed_releases) - correctness_count}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    sys.exit(main())\n","numLines":165,"startLine":1,"totalLines":165}}}

Set user agent for GitHub API requirements\n            headers = {\n                'User-Agent': f'{REPO_OWNER}-{REPO_NAME}-release-fetcher',\n                'Accept': 'application/vnd.github.v3+json'\n            }\n\n            req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers)\n\n            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:\n                if response.status != 200:\n                    print(f\"Error: GitHub API returned status {response.status}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                    sys.exit(1)\n\n                data = json.loads(response.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n                if not data:\n                    break\n\n                releases.extend(data)\n\n                # Check if we've fetched all releases\n                if len(data) < per_page:\n                    break\n\n                page += 1\n                # Rate limit avoidance: small delay between pages\n                time.sleep(0.1)\n\n        except urllib.error.URLError as e:\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            print(f\"Error decoding JSON response: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Unexpected error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n\n    return releases\n\n\ndef is_correctness_release(release: dict) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Determine if a release is correctness-labeled based on tag name and release notes.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        True if release appears to be correctness/security-related\n    \"\"\"\n    tag_name = release.get('tag_name', '').lower()\n    name = release.get('name', '').lower()\n    body = release.get('body', '').lower()\n\n    # Check both tag name, release name, and body for correctness keywords\n    combined_text = f\"{tag_name} {name} {body}\"\n\n    for keyword in CORRECTNESS_KEYWORDS:\n        if keyword in combined_text:\n            return True\n\n    return False\n\n\ndef parse_release(release: dict) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a GitHub release into structured output.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary with {tag, published_at, is_correctness, url}\n    \"\"\"\n    return {\n        'tag': release.get('tag_name', ''),\n        'published_at': release.get('published_at', ''),\n        'is_correctness': is_correctness_release(release),\n        'url': release.get('html_url', ''),\n    }\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Main entry point.\"\"\"\n    # Fetch all releases\n    raw_releases = fetch_releases()\n\n    if not raw_releases:\n        print(\"No releases found\", file=sys.stderr)\n        print(json.dumps([], indent=2))\n        return 0\n\n    # Parse releases\n    parsed_releases = [parse_release(r) for r in raw_releases]\n\n    # Output as JSON (already sorted by GitHub API - newest first)\n    print(json.dumps(parsed_releases, indent=2))\n\n    # Print summary to stderr\n    correctness_count = sum(1 for r in parsed_releases if r['is_correctness'])\n    print(f\"Fetched {len(parsed_releases)} release(s)\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"Correctness-labeled: {correctness_count}\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"Routine version bumps: {len(parsed_releases) - correctness_count}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    sys.exit(main())\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":4,"oldLines":13,"newStart":4,"newLines":22,"lines":[" from routine version bumps."," "," Returns structured JSON: [{tag, published_at, is_correctness, url}]","+","+Features:","+- Fetches latest N releases from GitHub API","+- Identifies correctness-labeled releases via keyword detection","+- Local caching with TTL to avoid API spam","+- Graceful rate limit handling"," \"\"\""," "," import json","+import os"," import sys"," import time"," import urllib.error"," import urllib.request","+from datetime import datetime, timedelta","+from pathlib import Path"," "," "," # import Path\n\n\n# Configuration\nREPO_OWNER = \"jedarden\"\nREPO_NAME = \"ARMOR\"\nGITHUB_API_URL = f\"https://api.github.com/repos/{REPO_OWNER}/{REPO_NAME}/releases\"\n\n# Cache configuration\nCACHE_DIR = Path.home() / '.cache' / 'armor-release-fetcher'\nCACHE_FILE = CACHE_DIR / 'releases.json'\nCACHE_TTL_SECONDS = 3600  # 1 hour default TTL\n\n# Keywords that indicate per_page: Number of releases per page (max 100)\n\n    Returns:\n        List of release dictionaries from GitHub API\n    \"\"\"\n    releases = []\n    page = 1\n\n    while True:\n        try:\n            url = f\"{GITHUB_API_URL}?per_page={per_page}&page={page}\"\n\n            # Set user agent for GitHub API requirements\n            headers = {\n                'User-Agent': f'{REPO_OWNER}-{REPO_NAME}-release-fetcher',\n                'Accept': 'application/vnd.github.v3+json'\n            }\n\n            req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers)\n\n            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:\n                if response.status != 200:\n                    print(f\"Error: GitHub API returned status {response.status}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                    sys.exit(1)\n\n                data = json.loads(response.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n                if not data:\n                    break\n\n                releases.extend(data)\n\n                # Check if we've fetched all releases\n                if len(data) < per_page:\n                    break\n\n                page += 1\n                # Rate limit avoidance: small delay between pages\n                time.sleep(0.1)\n\n        except urllib.error.URLError as e:\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n      print(f\"Unexpected error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n\n    return releases\n\n\ndef is_correctness_release(release: dict) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Determine if a release is correctness-labeled based on tag name and release notes.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        True if release appears to be correctness/security-related\n    \"\"\"\n    tag_name = release.get('tag_name', '').lower()\n    name = release.get('name', '').lower()\n    body = release.get('body', '').lower()\n\n    # Check both tag name, release name, and body for correctness keywords\n    combined_text = f\"{tag_name} {name} {body}\"\n\n    for keyword in CORRECTNESS_KEYWORDS:\n        if keyword in combined_text:\n            return True\n\n    return False\n\n\ndef parse_release(release: dict) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a GitHub release into structured output.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release 0\n\n    # Parse releases\n    parsed_releases = [parse_release(r) for r in raw_releases]\n\n    # Output as JSON (already sorted by GitHub API - newest first)\n    print(json.dumps(parsed_releases, Check cache first (unless force refresh is requested)\n    if use_cache and not force_refresh:\n        cached = load_cached_releases()\n        if cached is not None:\n            # Apply limit if specified\n            if limit is not None and len(cached) > limit:\n                return cached[:limit]\n            return cached\n\n    print(\"Fetching releases from GitHub API...\", file=sys.stderr)\n    releases = []\n    page = 1\n\n    while True:\n        try:\n            url = f\"{GITHUB_API_URL}?per_page={per_page}&page={page}\"\n\n            # Set user agent for GitHub API requirements\n            headers = {\n                'User-Agent': f'{REPO_OWNER}-{REPO_NAME}-release-fetcher',\n                'Accept': 'application/vnd.github.v3+json'\n            }\n\n            req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers)\n\n            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:\n                if response.status != 200:\n                    print(f\"Error: GitHub API returned status {response.status}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                    sys.exit(1)\n\n                data = json.loads(response.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n                if not data:\n                    break\n\n                releases.extend(data)\n\n                # Rate limit avoidance: small delay between pages\n                time.sleep(0.1)\n\n        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:\n            if e.code == 403:\n                # Rate limit exceeded\n                print(f\"Error: GitHub API rate limit exceeded. \"\n                      f\"Use --cache to avoid repeated API calls.\", file=sys.stderr)\n                sys.exit(1)\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except urllib.error.URLError as e:\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            print(f\"Error decoding JSON response: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Unexpected error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n\n    return releases","originalFile":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nFetch ARMOR releases from GitHub API and distinguish correctness-labeled releases\nfrom routine version bumps.\n\nReturns structured JSON: [{tag, published_at, is_correctness, url}]\n\nFeatures:\n- Fetches latest N releases from GitHub API\n- Identifies correctness-labeled releases via keyword detection\n- Local caching with TTL to avoid API spam\n- Graceful rate limit handling\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport time\nimport urllib.error\nimport urllib.request\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n\n# Configuration\nREPO_OWNER = \"jedarden\"\nREPO_NAME = \"ARMOR\"\nGITHUB_API_URL = f\"https://api.github.com/repos/{REPO_OWNER}/{REPO_NAME}/releases\"\n\n# Cache configuration\nCACHE_DIR = Path.home() / '.cache' / 'armor-release-fetcher'\nCACHE_FILE = CACHE_DIR / 'releases.json'\nCACHE_TTL_SECONDS = 3600  # 1 hour default TTL\n\n# Keywords that indicate correctness/security releases\nCORRECTNESS_KEYWORDS = [\n    'correctness',\n    'fix',\n    'critical',\n    'security',\n    'bug',\n    'patch',\n    'hotfix',\n    'urgent',\n    'vulnerability',\n    'cve',\n    'issue',\n    'regression',\n]\n\n\ndef is_cache_valid() -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Check if cached data exists and is within TTL.\n\n    Returns:\n        True if cache is valid, False otherwise\n    \"\"\"\n    if not CACHE_FILE.exists():\n        return False\n\n    try:\n        # Check cache age\n        cache_mtime = datetime.fromtimestamp(CACHE_FILE.stat().st_mtime)\n        age = datetime.now() - cache_mtime\n\n        return age.total_seconds() < CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n    except Exception:\n        return False\n\n\ndef load_cached_releases() -> list[dict] | None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Load releases from cache if valid.\n\n    Returns:\n        List of cached releases or None if cache is invalid/missing\n    \"\"\"\n    if not is_cache_valid():\n        return None\n\n    try:\n        with open(CACHE_FILE, 'r') as f:\n            cache_data = json.load(f)\n\n        # Validate cache structure\n        if not isinstance(cache_data, dict):\n            return None\n\n        if 'releases' not in cache_data or 'cached_at' not in cache_data:\n            return None\n\n        cached_at = datetime.fromisoformat(cache_data['cached_at'])\n        age = datetime.now() - cached_at\n\n        if age.total_seconds() >= CACHE_TTL_SECONDS:\n            return None\n\n        print(f\"Using cached data from {cached_at.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')} (age: {int(age.total_seconds())}s)\",\n              file=sys.stderr)\n        return cache_data['releases']\n\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError, ValueError) as e:\n        print(f\"Warning: Invalid cache file: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return None\n\n\ndef save_releases_to_cache(releases: list[dict]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Save releases to cache with timestamp.\n\n    Args:\n        releases: List of parsed release dictionaries\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Create cache directory if it doesn't exist\n        CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        cache_data = {\n            'cached_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),\n            'releases': releases,\n            'repo': f\"{REPO_OWNER}/{REPO_NAME}\",\n            'ttl_seconds': CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n        }\n\n        with open(CACHE_FILE, 'w') as f:\n            json.dump(cache_data, f, indent=2)\n\n        print(f\"Cached {len(releases)} release(s) to {CACHE_FILE}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"Warning: Failed to write cache: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n\ndef clear_cache() -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Clear the release cache.\n\n    Returns:\n        True if cache was cleared, False if it didn't exist\n    \"\"\"\n    if CACHE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            CACHE_FILE.unlink()\n            print(f\"Cleared cache at {CACHE_FILE}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Error clearing cache: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return False\n    return False\n\n\ndef fetch_releases(per_page: int = 100, use_cache: bool = True, force_refresh: bool = False) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fetch all releases from GitHub API.\n\n    Args:\n        per_page: Number of releases per page (max 100)\n\n    Returns:\n        List of release dictionaries from GitHub API\n    \"\"\"\n    releases = []\n    page = 1\n\n    while True:\n        try:\n            url = f\"{GITHUB_API_URL}?per_page={per_page}&page={page}\"\n\n            # Set user agent for GitHub API requirements\n            headers = {\n                'User-Agent': f'{REPO_OWNER}-{REPO_NAME}-release-fetcher',\n                'Accept': 'application/vnd.github.v3+json'\n            }\n\n            req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers)\n\n            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:\n                if response.status != 200:\n                    print(f\"Error: GitHub API returned status {response.status}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                    sys.exit(1)\n\n                data = json.loads(response.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n                if not data:\n                    break\n\n                releases.extend(data)\n\n                # Check if we've fetched all releases\n                if len(data) < per_page:\n                    break\n\n                page += 1\n                # Rate limit avoidance: small delay between pages\n                time.sleep(0.1)\n\n        except urllib.error.URLError as e:\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            print(f\"Error decoding JSON response: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Unexpected error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n\n    return releases\n\n\ndef is_correctness_release(release: dict) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Determine if a release is correctness-labeled based on tag name and release notes.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        True if release appears to be correctness/security-related\n    \"\"\"\n    tag_name = release.get('tag_name', '').lower()\n    name = release.get('name', '').lower()\n    body = release.get('body', '').lower()\n\n    # Check both tag name, release name, and body for correctness keywords\n    combined_text = f\"{tag_name} {name} {body}\"\n\n    for keyword in CORRECTNESS_KEYWORDS:\n        if keyword in combined_text:\n            return True\n\n    return False\n\n\ndef parse_release(release: dict) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a GitHub release into structured output.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary with {tag, published_at, is_correctness, url}\n    \"\"\"\n    return {\n        'tag': release.get('tag_name', ''),\n        'published_at': release.get('published_at', ''),\n        'is_correctness': is_correctness_release(release),\n        'url': release.get('html_url', ''),\n    }\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Main entry point.\"\"\"\n    # Fetch all releases\n    raw_releases = fetch_releases()\n\n    if not raw_releases:\n        print(\"No releases found\", file=sys.stderr)\n        print(json.dumps([], indent=2))\n        return 0\n\n    # Parse releases\n    parsed_releases = [parse_release(r) for r in raw_releases]\n\n    # Output as JSON (already sorted by GitHub API - newest first)\n    print(json.dumps(parsed_releases, indent=2))\n\n    # Print summary to stderr\n    correctness_count = sum(1 for r in parsed_releases if r['is_correctness'])\n    print(f\"Fetched {len(parsed_releases)} release(s)\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"Correctness-labeled: {correctness_count}\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"Routine version bumps: {len(parsed_releases) - correctness_count}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    sys.exit(main())\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":150,"oldLines":16,"newStart":150,"newLines":29,"lines":["     return False"," "," ","-def fetch_releases(per_page: int = 100, use_cache: bool = True, force_refresh: bool = False) -> list[dict]:","+def fetch_releases(per_page: int = 100, use_cache: bool = True, force_refresh: bool = False, limit: int | None = None) -> list[dict]:","     \"\"\"","-    Fetch all releases from GitHub API.","+    Fetch releases from GitHub API with optional caching."," ","     Args:","         per_page: Number of releases per page (max 100)","+        use_cache: Whether to use cached data if available","+        force_refresh: Force refresh from GitHub API even if cache is valid","+        limit: Maximum number of releases to fetch (None for all)"," ","     Returns:","         List of release dictionaries from GitHub API","     \"\"\"","+    # 1 hour default TTL\n\n# Keywords that indicate correctness/security releases\nCORRECTNESS_KEYWORDS = [\n    'correctness',\n    'fix',\n    'critical',\n    'security',\n    'bug',\n    'patch',\n    'hotfix',\n    'urgent',\n    'vulnerability',\n    'cve',\n    'issue',\n    'regression',\n]\n\n\ndef is_cache_valid() -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Check if cached data exists and is within TTL.\n\n    Returns:\n        True if cache is valid, False otherwise\n    \"\"\"\n    if not CACHE_FILE.exists():\n        return False\n\n    try:\n        # Check cache age\n        cache_mtime = datetime.fromtimestamp(CACHE_FILE.stat().st_mtime)\n        age = datetime.now() - cache_mtime\n\n        return age.total_seconds() < CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n    except Exception:\n        return False\n\n\ndef load_cached_releases() -> list[dict] | None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Load releases from cache if valid.\n\n    Returns:\n        List of cached releases or None if cache is invalid/missing\n    \"\"\"\n    if not is_cache_valid():\n        return None\n\n    try:\n        with open(CACHE_FILE, 'r') as f:\n            cache_data = json.load(f)\n\n        # Validate cache structure\n        if not isinstance(cache_data, dict):\n            return None\n\n        if 'releases' not in cache_data or 'cached_at' not in cache_data:\n            return None\n\n        cached_at = datetime.fromisoformat(cache_data['cached_at'])\n        age = datetime.now() - cached_at\n\n        if age.total_seconds() >= CACHE_TTL_SECONDS:\n            return None\n\n        print(f\"Using cached data from {cached_at.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')} (age: {int(age.total_seconds())}s)\",\n              file=sys.stderr)\n        return cache_data['releases']\n\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError, ValueError) as e:\n        print(f\"Warning: Invalid cache file: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return None\n\n\ndef save_releases_to_cache(releases: list[dict]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Save releases to cache with timestamp.\n\n    Args:\n        releases: List of parsed release dictionaries\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Create cache directory if it doesn't exist\n        CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        cache_data = {\n            'cached_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),\n            'releases': releases,\n            'repo': f\"{REPO_OWNER}/{REPO_NAME}\",\n            'ttl_seconds': CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n        }\n\n        with open(CACHE_FILE, 'w') as f:\n            json.dump(cache_data, f, indent=2)\n\n        print(f\"Cached {len(releases)} release(s) to {CACHE_FILE}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"Warning: Failed to write cache: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n\ndef clear_cache() -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Clear the release cache.\n\n    Returns:\n        True if cache was cleared, False if it didn't exist\n    \"\"\"\n    if CACHE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            CACHE_FILE.unlink()\n            print(f\"Cleared cache at {CACHE_FILE}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Error clearing cache: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return False\n    return False\n\n\ndef fetch_releases(per_page: int = 100, use_cache: bool = True, force_refresh: bool = False, limit: int | None = None) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fetch releases from GitHub API with optional caching.\n\n    Args:\n        per_page: Number of releases per page (max 100)\n        use_cache: Whether to use cached data if available\n        force_refresh: Force refresh from GitHub API even if cache is valid\n        limit: Maximum number of releases to fetch (None for all)\n\n    Returns:\n        List of release dictionaries from GitHub API\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check cache first (unless force refresh is requested)\n    if use_cache and not force_refresh:\n        cached = load_cached_releases()\n        if cached is not None:\n            # Apply limit if specified\n            if limit is not None and len(cached) > limit:\n                return cached[:limit]\n            return cached\n\n    print(\"Fetching releases from GitHub API...\", file=sys.stderr)\n    releases = []\n    page = 1\n\n    while True:\n        try:\n            url = f\"{GITHUB_API_URL}?per_page={per_page}&page={page}\"\n\n            # Set user agent for GitHub API requirements\n            headers = {\n                'User-Agent': f'{REPO_OWNER}-{REPO_NAME}-release-fetcher',\n                'Accept': 'application/vnd.github.v3+json'\n            }\n\n            req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers)\n\n            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:\n                if response.status != 200:\n                    print(f\"Error: GitHub API returned status {response.status}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                    sys.exit(1)\n\n                data = json.loads(response.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n                if not data:\n                    break\n\n                releases.extend(data)\n\n                # Check if we've reached the limit\n                if limit is not None and len(releases) >= limit:\n                    releases = releases[:limit]\n                    break\n\n                # Check if we've fetched all releases\n                if len(data) < per_page:\n                    break\n\n                page += 1\n                # Rate limit avoidance: small delay between pages\n                time.sleep(0.1)\n\n        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:\n            if e.code == 403:\n                # Rate limit exceeded\n                print(f\"Error: GitHub API rate limit exceeded. \"\n                      f\"Use --cache to avoid repeated API calls.\", file=sys.stderr)\n                sys.exit(1)\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except urllib.error.URLError as e:\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            print(f\"Error decoding JSON response: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Unexpected error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n\n    return releases\n\n\ndef is_correctness_release(release: dict) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Determine if a release is correctness-labeled based on tag name and release notes.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        True if release appears to be correctness/security-related\n    \"\"\"\n    tag_name = release.get('tag_name', '').lower()\n    name = release.get('name', '').lower()\n    body = release.get('body', '').lower()\n\n    # Check both tag name, release name, and body for correctness keywords\n    combined_text = f\"{tag_name} {name} {body}\"\n\n    for keyword in CORRECTNESS_KEYWORDS:\n        if keyword in combined_text:\n            return True\n\n    return False\n\n\ndef parse_release(release: dict) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a GitHub release into structured output.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary with {tag, published_at, is_correctness, url}\n    \"\"\"\n    return {\n        'tag': release.get('tag_name', ''),\n        'published_at': release.get('published_at', ''),\n        'is_correctness': is_correctness_release(release),\n        'url': release.get('html_url', ''),\n    }\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Main entry point.\"\"\"\n    # Fetch all releases\n    raw_releases = fetch_releases()\n\n    if not raw_releases:\n        print(\"No releases found\", file=sys.stderr)\n        print(json.dumps([], indent=2))\n        return 0\n\n    # Parse releases\n    parsed_releases = [parse_release(r) for r in raw_releases]\n\n    # Output as JSON (already sorted by GitHub API - newest first)\n    print(json.dumps(parsed_releases, indent=2))\n\n    # Print summary to stderr\n    correctness_count = sum(1 for r in parsed_releases if r['is_correctness'])\n    print(f\"Fetched {len(parsed_releases)} release(s)\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"Correctness-labeled: {correctness_count}\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"Routine version bumps: {len(parsed_releases) - correctness_count}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    sys.exit(main())\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":278,"oldLines":9,"newStart":278,"newLines":72,"lines":[" "," def main():","     \"\"\"Main entry point.\"\"\"","-    # Check cache age\n        cache_mtime = datetime.fromtimestamp(CACHE_FILE.stat().st_mtime)\n        age = datetime.now() - cache_mtime\n\n        return age.total_seconds() < CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n    except Exception:\n        return False\n\n\ndef load_cached_releases() -> list[dict] | None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Load releases from cache if valid.\n\n    Returns:\n        List of cached releases or None if cache is invalid/missing\n    \"\"\"\n    if not is_cache_valid():\n        return None\n\n    try:\n        with open(CACHE_FILE, 'r') as f:\n            cache_data = json.load(f)\n\n        # Validate cache structure\n        if not isinstance(cache_data, dict):\n            return None\n\n        if 'releases' not in cache_data or 'cached_at' not in cache_data:\n            return None\n\n        cached_at = datetime.fromisoformat(cache_data['cached_at'])\n        age = datetime.now() - cached_at\n\n        if age.total_seconds() >= CACHE_TTL_SECONDS:\n            return None\n\n        print(f\"Using cached data from {cached_at.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')} (age: {int(age.total_seconds())}s)\",\n              file=sys.stderr)\n        return cache_data['releases']\n\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError, ValueError) as e:\n        print(f\"Warning: Invalid cache file: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return None\n\n\ndef save_releases_to_cache(releases: list[dict]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Save releases to cache with timestamp.\n\n    Args:\n        releases: List of parsed release dictionaries\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Create cache directory if it doesn't exist\n        CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        cache_data = {\n            'cached_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),\n            'releases': releases,\n            'repo': f\"{REPO_OWNER}/{REPO_NAME}\",\n            'ttl_seconds': CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n        }\n\n        with open(CACHE_FILE, 'w') as f:\n            json.dump(cache_data, f, indent=2)\n\n        print(f\"Cached {len(releases)} release(s) to {CACHE_FILE}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"Warning: Failed to write cache: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n\ndef clear_cache() -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Clear the release cache.\n\n    Returns:\n        True if cache was cleared, False if it didn't exist\n    \"\"\"\n    if CACHE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            CACHE_FILE.unlink()\n            print(f\"Cleared cache at {CACHE_FILE}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Error clearing cache: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return False\n    return False\n\n\ndef fetch_releases(per_page: int = 100, use_cache: bool = True, force_refresh: bool = False, limit: int | None = None) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fetch releases from GitHub API with optional caching.\n\n    Args:\n        per_page: Number of releases per page (max 100)\n        use_cache: Whether to use cached data if available\n        force_refresh: Force refresh from GitHub API even if cache is valid\n        limit: Maximum number of releases to fetch (None for all)\n\n    Returns:\n        List of release dictionaries from GitHub API\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check cache first (unless force refresh is requested)\n    if use_cache and not force_refresh:\n        cached = load_cached_releases()\n        if cached is not None:\n            # Apply limit if specified\n            if limit is not None and len(cached) > limit:\n                return cached[:limit]\n            return cached\n\n    print(\"Fetching releases from GitHub API...\", file=sys.stderr)\n    releases = []\n    page = 1\n\n    while True:\n        try:\n            url = f\"{GITHUB_API_URL}?per_page={per_page}&page={page}\"\n\n            # Set user agent for GitHub API requirements\n            headers = {\n                'User-Agent': f'{REPO_OWNER}-{REPO_NAME}-release-fetcher',\n                'Accept': 'application/vnd.github.v3+json'\n            }\n\n            req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers)\n\n            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:\n                if response.status != 200:\n                    print(f\"Error: GitHub API returned status {response.status}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                    sys.exit(1)\n\n                data = json.loads(response.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n                if not data:\n                    break\n\n                releases.extend(data)\n\n                # Check if we've reached the limit\n                if limit is not None and len(releases) >= limit:\n                    releases = releases[:limit]\n                    break\n\n                # Check if we've fetched all releases\n                if len(data) < per_page:\n                    break\n\n                page += 1\n                # Rate limit avoidance: small delay between pages\n                time.sleep(0.1)\n\n        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:\n            if e.code == 403:\n                # Rate limit exceeded\n                print(f\"Error: GitHub API rate limit exceeded. \"\n                      f\"Use --cache to avoid repeated API calls.\", file=sys.stderr)\n                sys.exit(1)\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except urllib.error.URLError as e:\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            print(f\"Error decoding JSON response: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Unexpected error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n\n    return releases\n\n\ndef is_correctness_release(release: dict) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Determine if a release is correctness-labeled based on tag name and release notes.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        True if release appears to be correctness/security-related\n    \"\"\"\n    tag_name = release.get('tag_name', '').lower()\n    name = release.get('name', '').lower()\n    body = release.get('body', '').lower()\n\n    # Check both tag name, release name, and body for correctness keywords\n    combined_text = f\"{tag_name} {name} {body}\"\n\n    for keyword in CORRECTNESS_KEYWORDS:\n        if keyword in combined_text:\n            return True\n\n    return False\n\n\ndef parse_release(release: dict) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a GitHub release into structured output.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary with {tag, published_at, is_correctness, url}\n    \"\"\"\n    return {\n        'tag': release.get('tag_name', ''),\n        'published_at': release.get('published_at', ''),\n        'is_correctness': is_correctness_release(release),\n        'url': release.get('html_url', ''),\n    }\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Main entry point.\"\"\"\n    import argparse\n\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n        description='Fetch ARMOR releases from GitHub API',\n        epilog='Examples:\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s                 # Fetch all releases (uses cache if fresh)\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --limit 10      # Fetch latest 10 releases\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --refresh       # Force refresh from GitHub API\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --no-cache       # Bypass cache entirely\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --clear-cache    # Clear cached data and exit',\n        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter\n    )\n\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--limit', '-n',\n        type=int,\n        help='Maximum number of releases to fetch (default: all)'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--refresh',\n        action='store_true',\n        help='Force refresh from GitHub API (ignore cache)'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--no-cache',\n        action='store_true',\n        help='Disable cache reading/writing'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--clear-cache',\n        action='store_true',\n        help='Clear cached data and exit'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--cache-ttl',\n        type=int,\n        help=f'Override cache TTL in seconds (default: {CACHE_TTL_SECONDS})'\n    )\n\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    # Handle cache TTL override\n    global CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n    if args.cache_ttl:\n        CACHE_TTL_SECONDS = args.cache_ttl\n\n    # Handle cache clearing\n    if args.clear_cache:\n        if clear_cache():\n            print(\"Cache cleared successfully\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return 0\n        else:\n            print(\"No cache to clear\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return 1\n\n    # Determine cache settings\n    use_cache = not args.no_cache\n    force_refresh = args.refresh\n\n    # Fetch releases\n    raw_releases = fetch_releases(\n        limit=args.limit,\n        use_cache=use_cache,\n        force_refresh=force_refresh\n    )\n\n    if not raw_releases:\n        print(\"No releases found\", file=sys.stderr)\n        print(json.dumps([], indent=2))\n        return 0\n\n    # 1 hour default TTL\n\n# Keywords that indicate correctness/security releases\nCORRECTNESS_KEYWORDS = [\n    'correctness',\n    'fix',\n    'critical',\n    'security',\n    'bug',\n    'patch',\n    'hotfix',\n    'urgent',\n    'vulnerability',\n    'cve',\n    'issue',\n    'regression',\n]\n\n\ndef is_cache_valid() -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Check if cached data exists and is within TTL.\n\n    Returns:\n        True if cache is valid, False otherwise\n    \"\"\"\n    if not CACHE_FILE.exists():\n        return False\n\n    try:\n        # Check cache age\n        cache_mtime = datetime.fromtimestamp(CACHE_FILE.stat().st_mtime)\n        age = datetime.now() - cache_mtime\n\n        return age.total_seconds() < CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n    except Exception:\n        return False\n\n\ndef load_cached_releases() -> list[dict] | None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Load releases from cache if valid.\n\n    Returns:\n        List of cached releases or None if cache is invalid/missing\n    \"\"\"\n    if not is_cache_valid():\n        return None\n\n    try:\n        with open(CACHE_FILE, 'r') as f:\n            cache_data = json.load(f)\n\n        # Validate cache structure\n        if not isinstance(cache_data, dict):\n            return None\n\n        if 'releases' not in cache_data or 'cached_at' not in cache_data:\n            return None\n\n        cached_at = datetime.fromisoformat(cache_data['cached_at'])\n        age = datetime.now() - cached_at\n\n        if age.total_seconds() >= CACHE_TTL_SECONDS:\n            return None\n\n        print(f\"Using cached data from {cached_at.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')} (age: {int(age.total_seconds())}s)\",\n              file=sys.stderr)\n        return cache_data['releases']\n\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError, ValueError) as e:\n        print(f\"Warning: Invalid cache file: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return None\n\n\ndef save_releases_to_cache(releases: list[dict]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Save releases to cache with timestamp.\n\n    Args:\n        releases: List of parsed release dictionaries\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Create cache directory if it doesn't exist\n        CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        cache_data = {\n            'cached_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),\n            'releases': releases,\n            'repo': f\"{REPO_OWNER}/{REPO_NAME}\",\n            'ttl_seconds': CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n        }\n\n        with open(CACHE_FILE, 'w') as f:\n            json.dump(cache_data, f, indent=2)\n\n        print(f\"Cached {len(releases)} release(s) to {CACHE_FILE}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"Warning: Failed to write cache: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n\ndef clear_cache() -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Clear the release cache.\n\n    Returns:\n        True if cache was cleared, False if it didn't exist\n    \"\"\"\n    if CACHE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            CACHE_FILE.unlink()\n            print(f\"Cleared cache at {CACHE_FILE}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Error clearing cache: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return False\n    return False\n\n\ndef fetch_releases(per_page: int = 100, use_cache: bool = True, force_refresh: bool = False, limit: int | None = None) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fetch releases from GitHub API with optional caching.\n\n    Args:\n        per_page: Number of releases per page (max 100)\n        use_cache: Whether to use cached data if available\n        force_refresh: Force refresh from GitHub API even if cache is valid\n        limit: Maximum number of releases to fetch (None for all)\n\n    Returns:\n        List of release dictionaries from GitHub API\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check cache first (unless force refresh is requested)\n    if use_cache and not force_refresh:\n        cached = load_cached_releases()\n        if cached is not None:\n            # Apply limit if specified\n            if limit is not None and len(cached) > limit:\n                return cached[:limit]\n            return cached\n\n    print(\"Fetching releases from GitHub API...\", file=sys.stderr)\n    releases = []\n    page = 1\n\n    while True:\n        try:\n            url = f\"{GITHUB_API_URL}?per_page={per_page}&page={page}\"\n\n            # Set user agent for GitHub API requirements\n            headers = {\n                'User-Agent': f'{REPO_OWNER}-{REPO_NAME}-release-fetcher',\n                'Accept': 'application/vnd.github.v3+json'\n            }\n\n            req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers)\n\n            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:\n                if response.status != 200:\n                    print(f\"Error: GitHub API returned status {response.status}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                    sys.exit(1)\n\n                data = json.loads(response.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n                if not data:\n                    break\n\n                releases.extend(data)\n\n                # Check if we've reached the limit\n                if limit is not None and len(releases) >= limit:\n                    releases = releases[:limit]\n                    break\n\n                # Check if we've fetched all releases\n                if len(data) < per_page:\n                    break\n\n                page += 1\n                # Rate limit avoidance: small delay between pages\n                time.sleep(0.1)\n\n        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:\n            if e.code == 403:\n                # Rate limit exceeded\n                print(f\"Error: GitHub API rate limit exceeded. \"\n                      f\"Use --cache to avoid repeated API calls.\", file=sys.stderr)\n                sys.exit(1)\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except urllib.error.URLError as e:\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            print(f\"Error decoding JSON response: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Unexpected error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n\n    return releases\n\n\ndef is_correctness_release(release: dict) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Determine if a release is correctness-labeled based on tag name and release notes.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        True if release appears to be correctness/security-related\n    \"\"\"\n    tag_name = release.get('tag_name', '').lower()\n    name = release.get('name', '').lower()\n    body = release.get('body', '').lower()\n\n    # Check both tag name, release name, and body for correctness keywords\n    combined_text = f\"{tag_name} {name} {body}\"\n\n    for keyword in CORRECTNESS_KEYWORDS:\n        if keyword in combined_text:\n            return True\n\n    return False\n\n\ndef parse_release(release: dict) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a GitHub release into structured output.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary with {tag, published_at, is_correctness, url}\n    \"\"\"\n    return {\n        'tag': release.get('tag_name', ''),\n        'published_at': release.get('published_at', ''),\n        'is_correctness': is_correctness_release(release),\n        'url': release.get('html_url', ''),\n    }\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Main entry point.\"\"\"\n    import argparse\n\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n        description='Fetch ARMOR releases from GitHub API',\n        epilog='Examples:\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s                 # Fetch all releases (uses cache if fresh)\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --limit 10      # Fetch latest 10 releases\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --refresh       # Force refresh from GitHub API\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --no-cache       # Bypass cache entirely\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --clear-cache    # Clear cached data and exit',\n        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter\n    )\n\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--limit', '-n',\n        type=int,\n        help='Maximum number of releases to fetch (default: all)'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--refresh',\n        action='store_true',\n        help='Force refresh from GitHub API (ignore cache)'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--no-cache',\n        action='store_true',\n        help='Disable cache reading/writing'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--clear-cache',\n        action='store_true',\n        help='Clear cached data and exit'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--cache-ttl',\n        type=int,\n        help=f'Override cache TTL in seconds (default: {CACHE_TTL_SECONDS})'\n    )\n\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    # Handle cache TTL override\n    cache_ttl = args.cache_ttl if args.cache_ttl else CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n\n    # Update global TTL for this run\n    if args.cache_ttl:\n        CACHE_TTL_SECONDS = args.cache_ttl\n\n    # Handle cache clearing\n    if args.clear_cache:\n        if clear_cache():\n            print(\"Cache cleared successfully\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return 0\n        else:\n            print(\"No cache to clear\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return 1\n\n    # Determine cache settings\n    use_cache = not args.no_cache\n    force_refresh = args.refresh\n\n    # Fetch releases\n    raw_releases = fetch_releases(\n        limit=args.limit,\n        use_cache=use_cache,\n        force_refresh=force_refresh\n    )\n\n    if not raw_releases:\n        print(\"No releases found\", file=sys.stderr)\n        print(json.dumps([], indent=2))\n        return 0\n\n    # Parse releases\n    parsed_releases = [parse_release(r) for r in raw_releases]\n\n    # Save to cache if we fetched from API and cache is enabled\n    if use_cache and not args.no_cache:\n        # Only cache if we actually fetched from API (not from existing cache)\n        if force_refresh or not is_cache_valid():\n            save_releases_to_cache(parsed_releases)\n\n    # Output as JSON (already sorted by GitHub API - newest first)\n    print(json.dumps(parsed_releases, indent=2))\n\n    # Print summary to stderr\n    correctness_count = sum(1 for r in parsed_releases if r['is_correctness'])\n    print(f\"Fetched {len(parsed_releases)} release(s)\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"Correctness-labeled: {correctness_count}\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"Routine version bumps: {len(parsed_releases) - correctness_count}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    sys.exit(main())\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":49,"oldLines":13,"newStart":49,"newLines":19,"lines":[" ]"," "," ","-def is_cache_valid() -> bool:","+def is_cache_valid(ttl_seconds: int = None) -> bool:","     \"\"\"","     Check if cached data exists and is within TTL."," ","+    Args:","+        ttl_seconds: Override TTL in seconds (uses CACHE_TTL_SECONDS if None)","+","     Returns:","         True if cache is valid, False otherwise","     \"\"\"","+    if ttl_seconds is None:","+        ttl_seconds = CACHE_TTL_SECONDS","+","     if not CACHE_FILE.exists():","         return False"," "]},{"oldStart":64,"oldLines":7,"newStart":70,"newLines":7,"lines":["         cache_mtime = datetime.fromtimestamp(CACHE_FILE.stat().st_mtime)","         age = datetime.now() - cache_mtime"," ","-        return age.total_seconds() < CACHE_TTL_SECONDS","+        return age.total_seconds() < ttl_seconds","     except Exception:","         return False"," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

1 hour default TTL\n\n# Keywords that indicate correctness/security releases\nCORRECTNESS_KEYWORDS = [\n    'correctness',\n    'fix',\n    'critical',\n    'security',\n    'bug',\n    'patch',\n    'hotfix',\n    'urgent',\n    'vulnerability',\n    'cve',\n    'issue',\n    'regression',\n]\n\n\ndef is_cache_valid(ttl_seconds: int = None) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Check if cached data exists and is within TTL.\n\n    Args:\n        ttl_seconds: Override TTL in seconds (uses CACHE_TTL_SECONDS if None)\n\n    Returns:\n        True if cache is valid, False otherwise\n    \"\"\"\n    if ttl_seconds is None:\n        ttl_seconds = CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n\n    if not CACHE_FILE.exists():\n        return False\n\n    try:\n        # Check cache age\n        cache_mtime = datetime.fromtimestamp(CACHE_FILE.stat().st_mtime)\n        age = datetime.now() - cache_mtime\n\n        return age.total_seconds() < ttl_seconds\n    except Exception:\n        return False\n\n\ndef load_cached_releases() -> list[dict] | None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Load releases from cache if valid.\n\n    Returns:\n        List of cached releases or None if cache is invalid/missing\n    \"\"\"\n    if not is_cache_valid():\n        return None\n\n    try:\n        with open(CACHE_FILE, 'r') as f:\n            cache_data = json.load(f)\n\n        # Validate cache structure\n        if not isinstance(cache_data, dict):\n            return None\n\n        if 'releases' not in cache_data or 'cached_at' not in cache_data:\n            return None\n\n        cached_at = datetime.fromisoformat(cache_data['cached_at'])\n        age = datetime.now() - cached_at\n\n        if age.total_seconds() >= CACHE_TTL_SECONDS:\n            return None\n\n        print(f\"Using cached data from {cached_at.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')} (age: {int(age.total_seconds())}s)\",\n              file=sys.stderr)\n        return cache_data['releases']\n\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError, ValueError) as e:\n        print(f\"Warning: Invalid cache file: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return None\n\n\ndef save_releases_to_cache(releases: list[dict]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Save releases to cache with timestamp.\n\n    Args:\n        releases: List of parsed release dictionaries\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Create cache directory if it doesn't exist\n        CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        cache_data = {\n            'cached_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),\n            'releases': releases,\n            'repo': f\"{REPO_OWNER}/{REPO_NAME}\",\n            'ttl_seconds': CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n        }\n\n        with open(CACHE_FILE, 'w') as f:\n            json.dump(cache_data, f, indent=2)\n\n        print(f\"Cached {len(releases)} release(s) to {CACHE_FILE}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"Warning: Failed to write cache: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n\ndef clear_cache() -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Clear the release cache.\n\n    Returns:\n        True if cache was cleared, False if it didn't exist\n    \"\"\"\n    if CACHE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            CACHE_FILE.unlink()\n            print(f\"Cleared cache at {CACHE_FILE}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Error clearing cache: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return False\n    return False\n\n\ndef fetch_releases(per_page: int = 100, use_cache: bool = True, force_refresh: bool = False, limit: int | None = None) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fetch releases from GitHub API with optional caching.\n\n    Args:\n        per_page: Number of releases per page (max 100)\n        use_cache: Whether to use cached data if available\n        force_refresh: Force refresh from GitHub API even if cache is valid\n        limit: Maximum number of releases to fetch (None for all)\n\n    Returns:\n        List of release dictionaries from GitHub API\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check cache first (unless force refresh is requested)\n    if use_cache and not force_refresh:\n        cached = load_cached_releases()\n        if cached is not None:\n            # Apply limit if specified\n            if limit is not None and len(cached) > limit:\n                return cached[:limit]\n            return cached\n\n    print(\"Fetching releases from GitHub API...\", file=sys.stderr)\n    releases = []\n    page = 1\n\n    while True:\n        try:\n            url = f\"{GITHUB_API_URL}?per_page={per_page}&page={page}\"\n\n            # Set user agent for GitHub API requirements\n            headers = {\n                'User-Agent': f'{REPO_OWNER}-{REPO_NAME}-release-fetcher',\n                'Accept': 'application/vnd.github.v3+json'\n            }\n\n            req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers)\n\n            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:\n                if response.status != 200:\n                    print(f\"Error: GitHub API returned status {response.status}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                    sys.exit(1)\n\n                data = json.loads(response.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n                if not data:\n                    break\n\n                releases.extend(data)\n\n                # Check if we've reached the limit\n                if limit is not None and len(releases) >= limit:\n                    releases = releases[:limit]\n                    break\n\n                # Check if we've fetched all releases\n                if len(data) < per_page:\n                    break\n\n                page += 1\n                # Rate limit avoidance: small delay between pages\n                time.sleep(0.1)\n\n        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:\n            if e.code == 403:\n                # Rate limit exceeded\n                print(f\"Error: GitHub API rate limit exceeded. \"\n                      f\"Use --cache to avoid repeated API calls.\", file=sys.stderr)\n                sys.exit(1)\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except urllib.error.URLError as e:\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            print(f\"Error decoding JSON response: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Unexpected error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n\n    return releases\n\n\ndef is_correctness_release(release: dict) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Determine if a release is correctness-labeled based on tag name and release notes.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        True if release appears to be correctness/security-related\n    \"\"\"\n    tag_name = release.get('tag_name', '').lower()\n    name = release.get('name', '').lower()\n    body = release.get('body', '').lower()\n\n    # Check both tag name, release name, and body for correctness keywords\n    combined_text = f\"{tag_name} {name} {body}\"\n\n    for keyword in CORRECTNESS_KEYWORDS:\n        if keyword in combined_text:\n            return True\n\n    return False\n\n\ndef parse_release(release: dict) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a GitHub release into structured output.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary with {tag, published_at, is_correctness, url}\n    \"\"\"\n    return {\n        'tag': release.get('tag_name', ''),\n        'published_at': release.get('published_at', ''),\n        'is_correctness': is_correctness_release(release),\n        'url': release.get('html_url', ''),\n    }\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Main entry point.\"\"\"\n    import argparse\n\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n        description='Fetch ARMOR releases from GitHub API',\n        epilog='Examples:\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s                 # Fetch all releases (uses cache if fresh)\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --limit 10      # Fetch latest 10 releases\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --refresh       # Force refresh from GitHub API\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --no-cache       # Bypass cache entirely\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --clear-cache    # Clear cached data and exit',\n        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter\n    )\n\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--limit', '-n',\n        type=int,\n        help='Maximum number of releases to fetch (default: all)'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--refresh',\n        action='store_true',\n        help='Force refresh from GitHub API (ignore cache)'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--no-cache',\n        action='store_true',\n        help='Disable cache reading/writing'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--clear-cache',\n        action='store_true',\n        help='Clear cached data and exit'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--cache-ttl',\n        type=int,\n        help=f'Override cache TTL in seconds (default: {CACHE_TTL_SECONDS})'\n    )\n\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    # Handle cache TTL override\n    cache_ttl = args.cache_ttl if args.cache_ttl else CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n\n    # Update global TTL for this run\n    if args.cache_ttl:\n        CACHE_TTL_SECONDS = args.cache_ttl\n\n    # Handle cache clearing\n    if args.clear_cache:\n        if clear_cache():\n            print(\"Cache cleared successfully\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return 0\n        else:\n            print(\"No cache to clear\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return 1\n\n    # Determine cache settings\n    use_cache = not args.no_cache\n    force_refresh = args.refresh\n\n    # Fetch releases\n    raw_releases = fetch_releases(\n        limit=args.limit,\n        use_cache=use_cache,\n        force_refresh=force_refresh\n    )\n\n    if not raw_releases:\n        print(\"No releases found\", file=sys.stderr)\n        print(json.dumps([], indent=2))\n        return 0\n\n    # Parse releases\n    parsed_releases = [parse_release(r) for r in raw_releases]\n\n    # Save to cache if we fetched from API and cache is enabled\n    if use_cache and not args.no_cache:\n        # Only cache if we actually fetched from API (not from existing cache)\n        if force_refresh or not is_cache_valid():\n            save_releases_to_cache(parsed_releases)\n\n    # Output as JSON (already sorted by GitHub API - newest first)\n    print(json.dumps(parsed_releases, indent=2))\n\n    # Print summary to stderr\n    correctness_count = sum(1 for r in parsed_releases if r['is_correctness'])\n    print(f\"Fetched {len(parsed_releases)} release(s)\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"Correctness-labeled: {correctness_count}\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"Routine version bumps: {len(parsed_releases) - correctness_count}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    sys.exit(main())\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":75,"oldLines":14,"newStart":75,"newLines":20,"lines":["         return False"," "," ","-def load_cached_releases() -> list[dict] | None:","+def load_cached_releases(ttl_seconds: int = None) -> list[dict] | None:","     \"\"\"","     Load releases from cache if valid."," ","+    Args:","+        ttl_seconds: Override TTL in seconds (uses CACHE_TTL_SECONDS if None)","+","     Returns:","         List of cached releases or None if cache is invalid/missing","     \"\"\"","-    if not is_cache_valid():","+    if ttl_seconds is None:","+        ttl_seconds = CACHE_TTL_SECONDS","+","+    if not is_cache_valid(ttl_seconds):","         return None"," ","     try:"]},{"oldStart":99,"oldLines":7,"newStart":105,"newLines":7,"lines":["         cached_at = datetime.fromisoformat(cache_data['cached_at'])","         age = datetime.now() - cached_at"," ","-        if age.total_seconds() >= CACHE_TTL_SECONDS:","+        if age.total_seconds() >= ttl_seconds:","             return None"," ","         print(f\"Using cached data from {cached_at.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')} (age: {int(age.total_seconds())}s)\","]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

1 hour default TTL\n\n# Keywords that indicate correctness/security releases\nCORRECTNESS_KEYWORDS = [\n    'correctness',\n    'fix',\n    'critical',\n    'security',\n    'bug',\n    'patch',\n    'hotfix',\n    'urgent',\n    'vulnerability',\n    'cve',\n    'issue',\n    'regression',\n]\n\n\ndef is_cache_valid(ttl_seconds: int = None) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Check if cached data exists and is within TTL.\n\n    Args:\n        ttl_seconds: Override TTL in seconds (uses CACHE_TTL_SECONDS if None)\n\n    Returns:\n        True if cache is valid, False otherwise\n    \"\"\"\n    if ttl_seconds is None:\n        ttl_seconds = CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n\n    if not CACHE_FILE.exists():\n        return False\n\n    try:\n        # Check cache age\n        cache_mtime = datetime.fromtimestamp(CACHE_FILE.stat().st_mtime)\n        age = datetime.now() - cache_mtime\n\n        return age.total_seconds() < ttl_seconds\n    except Exception:\n        return False\n\n\ndef load_cached_releases(ttl_seconds: int = None) -> list[dict] | None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Load releases from cache if valid.\n\n    Args:\n        ttl_seconds: Override TTL in seconds (uses CACHE_TTL_SECONDS if None)\n\n    Returns:\n        List of cached releases or None if cache is invalid/missing\n    \"\"\"\n    if ttl_seconds is None:\n        ttl_seconds = CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n\n    if not is_cache_valid(ttl_seconds):\n        return None\n\n    try:\n        with open(CACHE_FILE, 'r') as f:\n            cache_data = json.load(f)\n\n        # Validate cache structure\n        if not isinstance(cache_data, dict):\n            return None\n\n        if 'releases' not in cache_data or 'cached_at' not in cache_data:\n            return None\n\n        cached_at = datetime.fromisoformat(cache_data['cached_at'])\n        age = datetime.now() - cached_at\n\n        if age.total_seconds() >= ttl_seconds:\n            return None\n\n        print(f\"Using cached data from {cached_at.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')} (age: {int(age.total_seconds())}s)\",\n              file=sys.stderr)\n        return cache_data['releases']\n\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError, ValueError) as e:\n        print(f\"Warning: Invalid cache file: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return None\n\n\ndef save_releases_to_cache(releases: list[dict]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Save releases to cache with timestamp.\n\n    Args:\n        releases: List of parsed release dictionaries\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Create cache directory if it doesn't exist\n        CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        cache_data = {\n            'cached_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),\n            'releases': releases,\n            'repo': f\"{REPO_OWNER}/{REPO_NAME}\",\n            'ttl_seconds': CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n        }\n\n        with open(CACHE_FILE, 'w') as f:\n            json.dump(cache_data, f, indent=2)\n\n        print(f\"Cached {len(releases)} release(s) to {CACHE_FILE}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"Warning: Failed to write cache: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n\ndef clear_cache() -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Clear the release cache.\n\n    Returns:\n        True if cache was cleared, False if it didn't exist\n    \"\"\"\n    if CACHE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            CACHE_FILE.unlink()\n            print(f\"Cleared cache at {CACHE_FILE}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Error clearing cache: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return False\n    return False\n\n\ndef fetch_releases(per_page: int = 100, use_cache: bool = True, force_refresh: bool = False, limit: int | None = None) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fetch releases from GitHub API with optional caching.\n\n    Args:\n        per_page: Number of releases per page (max 100)\n        use_cache: Whether to use cached data if available\n        force_refresh: Force refresh from GitHub API even if cache is valid\n        limit: Maximum number of releases to fetch (None for all)\n\n    Returns:\n        List of release dictionaries from GitHub API\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check cache first (unless force refresh is requested)\n    if use_cache and not force_refresh:\n        cached = load_cached_releases()\n        if cached is not None:\n            # Apply limit if specified\n            if limit is not None and len(cached) > limit:\n                return cached[:limit]\n            return cached\n\n    print(\"Fetching releases from GitHub API...\", file=sys.stderr)\n    releases = []\n    page = 1\n\n    while True:\n        try:\n            url = f\"{GITHUB_API_URL}?per_page={per_page}&page={page}\"\n\n            # Set user agent for GitHub API requirements\n            headers = {\n                'User-Agent': f'{REPO_OWNER}-{REPO_NAME}-release-fetcher',\n                'Accept': 'application/vnd.github.v3+json'\n            }\n\n            req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers)\n\n            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:\n                if response.status != 200:\n                    print(f\"Error: GitHub API returned status {response.status}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                    sys.exit(1)\n\n                data = json.loads(response.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n                if not data:\n                    break\n\n                releases.extend(data)\n\n                # Check if we've reached the limit\n                if limit is not None and len(releases) >= limit:\n                    releases = releases[:limit]\n                    break\n\n                # Check if we've fetched all releases\n                if len(data) < per_page:\n                    break\n\n                page += 1\n                # Rate limit avoidance: small delay between pages\n                time.sleep(0.1)\n\n        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:\n            if e.code == 403:\n                # Rate limit exceeded\n                print(f\"Error: GitHub API rate limit exceeded. \"\n                      f\"Use --cache to avoid repeated API calls.\", file=sys.stderr)\n                sys.exit(1)\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except urllib.error.URLError as e:\n            print(f\"Error fetching releases: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            print(f\"Error decoding JSON response: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Unexpected error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            sys.exit(1)\n\n    return releases\n\n\ndef is_correctness_release(release: dict) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Determine if a release is correctness-labeled based on tag name and release notes.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        True if release appears to be correctness/security-related\n    \"\"\"\n    tag_name = release.get('tag_name', '').lower()\n    name = release.get('name', '').lower()\n    body = release.get('body', '').lower()\n\n    # Check both tag name, release name, and body for correctness keywords\n    combined_text = f\"{tag_name} {name} {body}\"\n\n    for keyword in CORRECTNESS_KEYWORDS:\n        if keyword in combined_text:\n            return True\n\n    return False\n\n\ndef parse_release(release: dict) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a GitHub release into structured output.\n\n    Args:\n        release: GitHub release dictionary\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary with {tag, published_at, is_correctness, url}\n    \"\"\"\n    return {\n        'tag': release.get('tag_name', ''),\n        'published_at': release.get('published_at', ''),\n        'is_correctness': is_correctness_release(release),\n        'url': release.get('html_url', ''),\n    }\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Main entry point.\"\"\"\n    import argparse\n\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n        description='Fetch ARMOR releases from GitHub API',\n        epilog='Examples:\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s                 # Fetch all releases (uses cache if fresh)\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --limit 10      # Fetch latest 10 releases\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --refresh       # Force refresh from GitHub API\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --no-cache       # Bypass cache entirely\\n'\n               '  %(prog)s --clear-cache    # Clear cached data and exit',\n        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter\n    )\n\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--limit', '-n',\n        type=int,\n        help='Maximum number of releases to fetch (default: all)'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--refresh',\n        action='store_true',\n        help='Force refresh from GitHub API (ignore cache)'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--no-cache',\n        action='store_true',\n        help='Disable cache reading/writing'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--clear-cache',\n        action='store_true',\n        help='Clear cached data and exit'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--cache-ttl',\n        type=int,\n        help=f'Override cache TTL in seconds (default: {CACHE_TTL_SECONDS})'\n    )\n\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    # Handle cache TTL override\n    cache_ttl = args.cache_ttl if args.cache_ttl else CACHE_TTL_SECONDS\n\n    # Update global TTL for this run\n    if args.cache_ttl:\n        CACHE_TTL_SECONDS = args.cache_ttl\n\n    # Handle cache clearing\n    if args.clear_cache:\n        if clear_cache():\n            print(\"Cache cleared successfully\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return 0\n        else:\n            print(\"No cache to clear\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return 1\n\n    # Determine cache settings\n    use_cache = not args.no_cache\n    force_refresh = args.refresh\n\n    # Fetch releases\n    raw_releases = fetch_releases(\n        limit=args.limit,\n        use_cache=use_cache,\n        force_refresh=force_refresh\n    )\n\n    if not raw_releases:\n        print(\"No releases found\", file=sys.stderr)\n        print(json.dumps([], indent=2))\n        return 0\n\n    # Parse releases\n    parsed_releases = [parse_release(r) for r in raw_releases]\n\n    # Save to cache if we fetched from API and cache is enabled\n    if use_cache and not args.no_cache:\n        # Only cache if we actually fetched from API (not from existing cache)\n        if force_refresh or not is_cache_valid():\n            save_releases_to_cache(parsed_releases)\n\n    # Output as JSON (already sorted by GitHub API - newest first)\n    print(json.dumps(parsed_releases, indent=2))\n\n    # Print summary to stderr\n    correctness_count = sum(1 for r in parsed_releases if r['is_correctness'])\n    print(f\"Fetched {len(parsed_releases)} release(s)\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"Correctness-labeled: {correctness_count}\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"Routine version bumps: {len(parsed_releases) - correctness_count}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    sys.exit(main())\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":162,"oldLines":7,"newStart":162,"newLines":7,"lines":["     return False"," "," ","-def fetch_releases(per_page: int = 100, use_cache: bool = True, force_refresh: bool = False, limit: int | None = None) -> list[dict]:","+def fetch_releases(per_page: int = 100, use_cache: bool = True, force_refresh: bool = False, limit: int | None = None, ttl_seconds: int = None) -> list[dict]:","     \"\"\"","     Fetch releases from GitHub API with optional caching."," "]},{"oldStart":171,"oldLines":13,"newStart":171,"newLines":14,"lines":["         use_cache: Whether to use cached data if available","         force_refresh: Force refresh from GitHub API even if cache is valid","         limit: Maximum number of releases to fetch (None for all)","+        ttl_seconds: Override cache TTL in seconds (uses CACHE_TTL_SECONDS if None)"," ","     Returns:","         List of release dictionaries from GitHub API","     \"\"\"","     #
